from the Northern Pacific and Great
Northern systems.


Railroad men say that If the head
traffic men of these lines condescend
to meet with the shippers here, and
it should come about that the ship
pers gain their point, it will mean
that other cities will follow suit and
likewise force the railways to con-
slider and grant such demands as may
be made.


A pooling of the freight interests of
this city has been rumored with an
intimation that, rather than give in
to the shippers, the Hill and the Har-
riman interests will concoct some
scheme to split the profits of Spokane
with the Q. r. g. N. 1t is
said that although the 0. r.. & N.
is being benefited at this time, 1t will
suffer later on should shippers in
other cities undertake such action as
have the shippers of this city.


In the meantime there is no truce
and will not be unless the railroad
men agree to come to Spokane.
